hypothesis: Enhancing steric complementarity with SSTR2-unique ECL2/TM5 residues by introducing bulky aromatic residues at position 5 and 6 will increase selectivity margin by differentiating SSTR2 from SSTR3/SSTR5.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs are achieving good selectivity but are structurally suboptimal. Tightening structural constraints and increasing candidate diversity will help maintain selectivity while improving structural quality.

proposed_changes.rationale: Structural failures are likely due to steric clashes in the designed peptides. Lowering the clash threshold will enforce better geometry and reduce structural failures.
The current number of candidates is low, which may limit the diversity of designs. Increasing the number of candidates will improve the chances of generating structurally valid and selective designs.
